speaker: S1
quote: No he needs full search Full search Yeah he's clenching his butt He's already searched him but he uh I think he's got something in
rationale: The speaker suggests a full search based on a suspicion that the individual may have something concealed, without clear evidence or consent. This could be seen as a violation of the Fourth Amendment, which protects against unreasonable searches and seizures.

speaker: S1
quote: Have you saw anything when you were getting pulled over You need to tell me now You're in control of that vehicle which means you're responsible for everything You ain't gonna make it right
rationale: The speaker's statement could be interpreted as intimidating, implying that the subject will not be able to 'make it right' unless they provide information. This could be seen as coercive and a violation of the Fifth Amendment right against self-incrimination.
